stem: A toy rocket is launched from ground level. The rocket reaches a maximum height of 256 feet above the ground at 4 seconds and hits the ground at 8 seconds. Which equation represents the height above the ground h, in feet, of the rocket t seconds after it is launched?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. A projectile under gravity has height h(t) = a(t − tmax)2 + hmax. Here tmax = 4 and hmax = 256, and the rocket hits the ground at t = 8, giving 0 = a(8 − 4)2 + 256, so a = −16. The equation is h = −16(t − 4)2 + 256.

Choices A and D drop the proper leading coefficient. Choice C uses the wrong sign on the maximum height.
